   "body_snippet": "When using Chrome remote desktop to connect to a windows 10 machine running mRemoteNG the right shift key isn't recognised (Tested in RDP)\r\n\r\n## Expected Behavior\r\nPressing the right shift key should send the key to the remote connection\r\n\r\n## Current Behavior\r\nNothing happens\r\n\r\n## Possible Solution\r\n<!--- Not obligatory, but suggest a fix/reason for the bug, -->\r\n<!--- or ideas how to implement the addition or change -->\r\n\r\n## Steps to Reproduce (for bugs)\r\n<!--- Provide an unambiguous set of ...",

-  "notes": ""
+  "notes": "AI triage (claude): Right shift key not recognized due to missing extended key flag handling in RDP keyboard input — right-side modifier keys require the extended key bit to distinguish them from their left counterparts.\nApproach: In the RDP keyboard event handler, check for extended key flag (e.KeyValue == 0xA1 or e.Modifiers check) to ensure right shift (VK_RSHIFT) is sent with the correct virtual key code rather than being collapsed into generic VK_SHIFT.",

diff --git a/mRemoteNG/Connection/Protocol/RDP/RdpProtocol.cs b/mRemoteNG/Connection/Protocol/RDP/RdpProtocol.cs
index 6b627a3ab..41062b1ab 100644
--- a/mRemoteNG/Connection/Protocol/RDP/RdpProtocol.cs
+++ b/mRemoteNG/Connection/Protocol/RDP/RdpProtocol.cs
@@ -1372,6 +1372,7 @@ namespace mRemoteNG.Connection.Protocol.RDP
         {
             try
             {
+                EnsureRightShiftKeyFixerRegistered();
                 _rdpClient.OnConnecting += RDPEvent_OnConnecting;
                 _rdpClient.OnConnected += RDPEvent_OnConnected;
                 _rdpClient.OnLoginComplete += RDPEvent_OnLoginComplete;
@@ -1387,7 +1388,48 @@ namespace mRemoteNG.Connection.Protocol.RDP
                 Runtime.MessageCollector.AddExceptionStackTrace(Language.RdpSetEventHandlersFailed, ex);
             }
         }
-        
+
+        // Fix for #1631: When mRemoteNG runs inside Chrome Remote Desktop (or similar remote
+        // access tools), right shift may be injected as WM_KEYDOWN with VK_SHIFT (0x10) and
+        // the right-shift scan code (0x36) in lParam rather than VK_RSHIFT (0xA1).  The RDP
+        // ActiveX client forwards the virtual-key code to the remote server, so it would send
+        // a generic VK_SHIFT and the remote host would not recognise the key as right shift.
+        // This one-time message filter re-posts such messages with the correct VK_RSHIFT code.
+        private static bool _rightShiftFixerRegistered;
+
+        private static void EnsureRightShiftKeyFixerRegistered()
+        {
+            if (_rightShiftFixerRegistered) return;
+            _rightShiftFixerRegistered = true;
+            Application.AddMessageFilter(new RightShiftKeyFixer());
+        }
+
+        private sealed class RightShiftKeyFixer : IMessageFilter
+        {
+            private const int VK_SHIFT = 0x10;
+            private const int VK_RSHIFT = 0xA1;
+            private const int RightShiftScanCode = 0x36;
+
+            public bool PreFilterMessage(ref System.Windows.Forms.Message m)
+            {
+                if ((m.Msg != NativeMethods.WM_KEYDOWN && m.Msg != NativeMethods.WM_KEYUP) ||
+                    (int)m.WParam != VK_SHIFT)
+                    return false;
+
+                // Scan code is in bits 16-23 of lParam; right shift = 0x36, left shift = 0x2A
+                int scanCode = (m.LParam.ToInt32() >> 16) & 0xFF;
+                if (scanCode != RightShiftScanCode)
+                    return false;
+
+                // Re-post the same message with VK_RSHIFT so the RDP ActiveX forwards the
+                // correct virtual key to the remote server.
+                IntPtr hwnd = m.HWnd != IntPtr.Zero ? m.HWnd : NativeMethods.GetForegroundWindow();
+                if (hwnd != IntPtr.Zero)
+                    NativeMethods.PostMessage(hwnd, m.Msg, (IntPtr)VK_RSHIFT, m.LParam);
+                return true; // consume the original VK_SHIFT message
+            }
+        }
+
         #endregion
 
         #region Private Events & Handlers
